responses.q1_note: I use AI for something every day. Whether it is to create an outline for something or to take an outline I've created and expand on it. I also use it regularly to ensure my communications are clear and concise.
responses.q1b_note: I use AI in my personal life about as much as I do at work. It has become an ideal thought partner, especially after training it over the last few years.
responses.q2a_note: I try to be as specific as possible in my prompts with any AI tool. I have developed a degree of specificity that has helped ensure I get the output I am looking for; sometimes, there are tweaks required, but more often than not, I get what I am looking for the first or second time around
responses.q3_note: Sometimes, I rewrite the prompt and start from scratch; more often than not, I just continue the conversation by clarifying what I am looking for.
responses.q4_note: I have used AI at work for several projects, where the output was used to present to our ELT. The results have been well received and used as we develop strategies tied to Learning and Development, Goals and Objectives, Performance Reviews, and Engagement Surveys.
responses.q5_note: Depending on the size of the ask, the project itself, and the type of results I am looking for, I have approached my interactions with AI in various ways. I have developed some best practices that seem to work well to produce the outcomes I need.
responses.q6a: I haven’t connected AI to other tools yet
responses.q6_note: This is what is next for me. I'd like to connect AI to my work tools, but connectivity in our ISO environment has proven to be a bit tricky.
responses.c1: I regularly use AI with documents/data in real workflows
c2: Verify key facts before using or sharing output
